questionHtml:
What is bonded to phosphates in a strand of RNA?


A.  Only carbohydrates

B.  Adenine, guanine, cytosine and thymine

C.  Adenine, guanine, cytosine and uracil

D.  Pentoses and bases

meta.title: What is bonded to phosphates in a strand of RNA? A. Only carbohydrates B. Adenine, guanine, cytosine and thymine C. Adenine, guanine, cytosine and uracil D. Pentoses and bases
breadcrumbLabels: A1.2.3. Sugar–phosphate bonding and the sugar–phosphate “backbone” of DNA and RNA
